rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Final Judgment Under Ccp 473(D), 366.2, 128(A), 187 Probate Code 9256, 9353, 1300, 1303, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration Of Camille Colarusso
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Petitioner Camile Colarusso's Motion to Vacate Final Judgment Under CCP 473(d), 366.2 [commencement of action after person's death. Limitations period], 128(a) [court's incidental powers], 187 [civil jurisdiction], Probate Code sections 9256, 9353, 1300, 1303. Per the Notice of Motion, Movant seeks an order vacating the Final Judgement entered on March 25, 2024 in case number CGC-22-599976, on various grounds: extrinsic fraud, lack of jurisdiction; time-barred creditor claims; violation of Probate Code sec. 9353 and 9256. The Motion is DENIED as procedurally defective. As Movant acknowledges in the moving papers, this court lacks jurisdiction to vacate the Final Judgment entered in the civil action CGC-22-599976. (See, "Petitioner understands that the Motion to vacate the final Judgment must be filed in the action in which the judgment was entered [citations]. Because the Civil Judgment at issue Was [sic] entered in the related civil action, only that Court may formally set aside or vacate the judgment under Code of Civil Procedure 473(d), 663, or 187." (Supp. at 1:21-25, attached to Notice of Errata filed 10/8.) The court declines the request made in the opposition that it issue "non-binding dicta" analyzing a motion it lacks jurisdiction to hear (Vigna Opp. MPA at 3:22). Movant shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL WRIGHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (3) A petition for final distribution must also state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (4) The report required when an account has been waived must list the information required by law, as set forth in CRC 7.550. (5) The Court will not sign orders where the last page includes only the signature line. [LRSF 14.7 A.5.] Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. All pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. [LRSF 14.15 A.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be addressed/cleared at the hearing: (1) The petition requests a waiver of publication; however, it lacks any supporting allegations. (2) Petition does not clarify whether the conservatee supports or opposes the sale, despite the petitioners having discussed the proposed sale with the conservatee, as required by Prbo. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HIMATLAL R. MEHTA AND JYOTSNA H. MEHTA REVOCABLE T TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 13, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code Section 17200 Et Seq.
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether venue in San Francisco is proper. Procedural issues with the petition: 1) The petition has no prayer. For the Court to make an order, the report and petition must include a prayer specifying the relief sought. 2) The proposed order received requests relief involving deadlines for summary judgment and the setting of an evidentiary hearing, neither of which were addressed or requested in the underlying petition. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DEBORAH JANE LINTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of closing the estate will be set for 5/3/27. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 5/3/27. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed. (NB: If a new notice of hearing must be served, proof of its service should just be filed. There is no need to narrate in a supplement that it will be done. A supplement or amendment to a petition does not need to highlight deficiencies by reciting them. Issues noted by an examiner are not part of the record unless set forth in a filed pleading; the additional information should just be provided in the supplement. The only correct form of notice in a decedent's estate is a Judicial Council form, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. Notice of a supplement does not need to be served (except for 850 petitions). [CRC 7.53(b).]
